bug.summary: Reopening a ticket that was CLOSED does not restart its SLA deadline (due_at). Reopening a ticket that was RESOLVED works correctly. Because closed -> open is a fully valid, common transition (per TicketController::ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS), the deadline silently goes stale for that path only.
bug.trigger: 1) Create a ticket (due_at gets computed at creation time as now + category.default_sla_hours). 2) Drive it to status 'closed' (e.g. open -> resolved -> closed, or directly persist status='closed' with an old due_at for the test). 3) Reopen it via POST /tickets/{id}/status with status=open (TicketController::changeStatus). Because the ticket's PRIOR status was 'closed', not 'resolved', the guard on line 136 (`if ($desired === 'open' && $ticket->status === 'resolved')`) is false, so due_at is never recomputed -- it is passed through unchanged from $ticket->dueAt (line 135) into TicketRepository::updateStatus(). If the ticket sat closed for any length of time, the persisted due_at is now in the past (already breached) even though the ticket is freshly reopened and active again. Reopening from 'resolved' (resolved -> open) is unaffected and correctly recomputes due_at from now.
bug.correct_behavior: Reopening a ticket -- from EITHER 'resolved' or 'closed' -- should restart the SLA clock: due_at should be recomputed as now + category.default_sla_hours, exactly like the resolved -> open path already does. Both status values represent 'this ticket had stopped actively being worked' and closed -> open is listed as an equally valid transition in ALLOWED_TRANSITIONS ('closed' => ['open']), so there is no basis for treating it differently.
bug.reference_fix: In TicketController::changeStatus, widen the condition on line 136 from `$ticket->status === 'resolved'` to `$ticket->status === 'resolved' || $ticket->status === 'closed'` (or equivalently `in_array($ticket->status, ['resolved', 'closed'], true)`), so the due_at recompute block runs for both prior statuses. No other files need to change; TicketRepository::updateStatus already accepts and persists the recomputed $dueAt.
bug.reference_regression_test: Add an Integration/Http test (see tests/Integration/Http/TicketControllerTest.php for the existing pattern/fixtures). Setup: seed fixtures via FixtureLoader::seedMinimal() for a requester/category, then directly insert a Ticket via TicketRepository::insert(...) with status='closed' and dueAt set to a stale value in the past (e.g. new DateTimeImmutable('-3 days')) -- this simulates a ticket that has been sitting closed since before its (already-passed) deadline. Action: call TicketController::changeStatus() (or POST /tickets/{id}/status) with parsed body ['status' => 'open'] against that ticket's id. Assertion: reload the ticket via TicketRepository::findById() and assert its dueAt is not null and is strictly greater than 'now' (new DateTimeImmutable()) -- i.e. the deadline was restarted, not left stale in the past. On the buggy branch this assertion fails because dueAt remains the injected past timestamp; after the one-line fix it passes because dueAt is recomputed to now + category default_sla_hours.
